title: Kill Effects
description: Kill Effects system for Paper/Spigot 1.21.1. Create custom cosmetic effects using YAML: particles, sounds, holograms, and geometry shapes.  permission-based GUI selection. by Jakuny
body:
# ⚔️ **KillEffects**
The ultimate cosmetic kill system for your PvP server.
KillEffects is a high-performance, fully customizable cosmetic plugin for Minecraft servers (Paper/Spigot 1.21.1). It allows your players to collect, showcase, and play spectacular visual and sound effects after defeating their opponents.
## 🌟 **Why choose KillEffects?**
Stop using boring, static kill messages! KillEffects transforms standard PvP into an engaging experience. Whether it's a simple lightning strike or a legendary holographic ascension, your players will love showing off their style.
## 💎 **For Server Owners**
YAML-Based Effect Engine: Create unlimited unique effects without touching a single line of Java code.
Advanced Visuals: Supports particles, sounds, lightning, circles, spirals, and even pixel-art particle text (e.g., "EZ").
Holographic Displays: Utilizes 1.21.1 native TextDisplay entities for smooth, lag-free holograms that face the player.
Dynamic UI: Beautiful, auto-generated GUI (/ke) where players can preview and select their effects.
Zero Lag: Optimized code built specifically for modern Paper/Spigot versions.
## 🎮 **Features for Players**
Easy Interface: Manage your aesthetics with one simple menu (/ke).
Personalization: Toggle visual effects or sounds separately.
Placeholders: Support for %victim% placeholder in holographic messages.
Collectibles: Unlock new effects through gameplay or store packages.
## 🛠 Admin Commands

```
/ke — Open the effects selection menu (killeffects.gui)
/ke toggle — Quickly enable/disable visual effects
/ke sound — Quickly enable/disable effect sounds
/ke reload — Refresh all configs and effects on the fly (killeffects.admin)
```

# 💡 How to create effects?
Creating an effect is as easy as creating a file in /plugins/KillEffects/effects/. Just define your sequence of actions (sounds, particles, text) in a .yml file and run /ke reload. Your players will see the new effect instantly! Also, on the project's GitHub there is a guide with all possible commands for creating your own effects.
